api/v3/handlers/featurecost.purpose: Single-operation v3 HTTP handler for querying feature cost by delegating to cost.Service using meter query params built via shared query.BuildQueryParams logic. Bridges feature lookup, meter resolution, and cost calculation into one endpoint with optional request body.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints
patterns.description: Use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs when the URL has a path parameter (featureID here). The third type param is the path param type (string). Plain httptransport.NewHandler is for body-only endpoints.
patterns.example: QueryFeatureCostHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[QueryFeatureCostRequest, QueryFeatureCostResponse, QueryFeatureCostParams]

patterns.name: Shared query param construction
patterns.description: Reuse query.BuildQueryParams from api/v3/handlers/meters/query for streaming query param construction rather than duplicating the logic. Pass a query.NewCustomerResolver wrapping customerService.
patterns.example: params, err := query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService))

patterns.name: Domain validation before service call
patterns.description: Validate domain preconditions (e.g., feature has a meter) in the operation func using models.NewGenericValidationError before calling the service.
patterns.example: if feat.MeterID == nil { return QueryFeatureCostResponse{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("feature %s has no meter associated", feat.Key)) }

patterns.name: Nil-safe conversion with empty slice fallback
patterns.description: ToAPIFeatureCostQueryResult returns an empty Data slice (not nil) when result is nil, preventing JSON null.
patterns.example: if result == nil { return api.FeatureCostQueryResult{..., Data: []api.FeatureCostQueryRow{}} }

patterns.name: ParseOptionalBody for query endpoints
patterns.description: Use request.ParseOptionalBody (not ParseBody) because the request body is optional for this query endpoint.
patterns.example: if err := request.ParseOptionalBody(r, &body); err != nil { return QueryFeatureCostRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: nullable.NewNullNullable vs nullable.NewNullableWithValue
patterns.description: In convert.go, use nullable.NewNullableWithValue when cost is present and nullable.NewNullNullable[api.Numeric]() when absent — wrong choice silently produces unexpected JSON nullability.
patterns.example: if row.Cost != nil { apiRow.Cost = nullable.NewNullableWithValue(row.Cost.String()) } else { apiRow.Cost = nullable.NewNullNullable[api.Numeric]() }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (one method: QueryFeatureCost) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, costService, featureConnector, meterService, customerService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All injected services must be present in New() signature; missing one causes Wire compile failure.

key_file_guides.role: Implements QueryFeatureCost operation: resolves namespace, parses optional body, looks up feature, validates meter association, builds query params, calls cost service.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ParseOptionalBody is used (not ParseBody) because request body is optional; switching to ParseBody breaks bodyless requests.

key_file_guides.role: Converts cost.CostQueryResult to api.FeatureCostQueryResult. Uses nullable.NewNullNullable for absent cost field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: nullable.NewNullNullable vs nullable.NewNullableWithValue must be chosen correctly; using wrong one silently produces unexpected JSON nullability.

anti_patterns: Duplicating query.BuildQueryParams logic inline instead of calling the shared function from api/v3/handlers/meters/query
Returning nil instead of empty slice for Data when result is nil — causes JSON null
Using context.Background() instead of propagating the request ctx
Calling request.ParseBody instead of request.ParseOptionalBody for this query endpoint

decisions.decision: Cost querying reuses meter query param builder (query.BuildQueryParams)
decisions.rationale: Feature cost uses the same streaming query structure as meter queries; sharing the builder avoids drift between the two filter/window handling paths.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a query operation with optional body and path param

api/v3/handlers/features.purpose: Full CRUD v3 HTTP handler package for product-catalog features (list, get, create, update, delete/archive). Includes LLM pricing enrichment on get/update responses and domain-error-specific HTTP status mapping via a local errorEncoder.

patterns.name: Per-operation file split
patterns.description: Each CRUD verb lives in its own file (create.go, get.go, list.go, update.go, delete.go). handler.go declares the Handler interface and New() constructor. convert.go holds all domain<->API type conversions. error_encoder.go holds domain-error HTTP status mapping.
patterns.example: func (h *handler) CreateFeature() CreateFeatureHandler { return httptransport.NewHandler(...) }

patterns.name: Goverter-free manual conversion with unit tests
patterns.description: This package uses hand-written convert.go functions (not Goverter) and covers them with convert_test.go. All convert functions return (T, error) to propagate parse failures.
patterns.example: func convertFeatureToAPI(f feature.Feature) (api.Feature, error)

patterns.name: Domain-specific errorEncoder chained after GenericErrorEncoder
patterns.description: Append httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()) after apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() in AppendOptions. errorEncoder handles feature-domain typed errors (FeatureInvalidFiltersError -> 400, ForbiddenError -> 403, FeatureWithNameAlreadyExistsError -> 409).
patterns.example: httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder())

patterns.name: Conditional LLM pricing enrichment on read responses
patterns.description: After converting a feature to API, check UnitCost.Type == UnitCostTypeLLM and h.llmcostService != nil before calling resolveLLMPricing. Pricing errors are silently swallowed — enrichment is best-effort. Done in both get.go and update.go.
patterns.example: if feat.UnitCost != nil && feat.UnitCost.Type == feature.UnitCostTypeLLM && h.llmcostService != nil { pricing := resolveLLMPricing(ctx, h.llmcostService, feat); if pricing != nil { enrichFeatureResponseWithPricing(&resp, pricing) } }

patterns.name: Meter validation at decode time
patterns.description: Meter ID resolution and filter key validation happen in the request decoder (first func of NewHandler), not in the operation func. Use models.NewGenericValidationError for filter key mismatches.
patterns.example: func validateMeterFilters(filters map[string]api.QueryFilterStringMapItem, m meter.Meter) error { for k := range filters { if _, ok := m.GroupBy[k]; !ok { return models.NewGenericValidationError(...) } } }

patterns.name: nullable.Nullable for optional-null PATCH fields
patterns.description: Update inputs use nullable.Nullable[T]; check IsNull() to clear, IsSpecified()+Get() to set, neither to leave unchanged. Test all three branches in convert_test.go.
patterns.example: if body.UnitCost.IsNull() { input.UnitCost = nullable.NewNullNullable[feature.UnitCost]() } else if body.UnitCost.IsSpecified() { ... }

patterns.name: UnitCost discriminated union via switch
patterns.description: convertUnitCostToAPI and convertUnitCostFromAPI use switch statements on UnitCostType/discriminator string. The default case must return an error for unknown types to catch future additions.
patterns.example: switch u.Type { case feature.UnitCostTypeManual: ... case feature.UnitCostTypeLLM: ... default: return out, fmt.Errorf("unknown unit cost type: %s", u.Type) }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface with 5 methods; handler struct holds resolveNamespace, connector (feature.FeatureConnector), meterService, llmcostService, options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: llmcostService may be nil (optional dependency); guard with != nil before calling in get.go and update.go.

key_file_guides.role: All domain<->API conversions: convertFeatureToAPI, convertCreateRequestToDomain, convertUpdateRequestToDomain, convertUnitCostToAPI/FromAPI, enrichFeatureResponseWithPricing, resolveLLMPricing, filter conversions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UnitCost discriminator switch must cover all UnitCostType values; unknown type returns error. resolveLLMPricing silently returns nil on error — callers must not treat nil as a hard failure.

key_file_guides.role: Maps feature-domain typed errors to HTTP status codes using commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new feature-domain errors here; do not let them fall through to 500 via unhandled types.

key_file_guides.role: List with pagination (default page 1/size 20), filter[meter_id], filter[key], filter[name], sort. Uses apierrors.NewBadRequestError for invalid filter/sort params.
key_file_guides.watch_for: page.Validate() must be called after construction; missing it allows page=0 through.

key_file_guides.role: Validates meter reference exists and filter keys match meter GroupBy dimensions before calling CreateFeature. Meter lookup and filter validation happen in the decoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: validateMeterFilters only checks key existence, not operator validity; operator validation is done downstream in MeterGroupByFilters.Validate.

anti_patterns: Calling h.connector directly from handler.go methods instead of delegating to per-operation files
Skipping errorEncoder() in AppendOptions — feature domain errors (FeatureInvalidFiltersError, ForbiddenError, FeatureWithNameAlreadyExistsError) will become 500
Returning pricing enrichment errors instead of silently skipping — pricing resolution is best-effort
Accepting llmcostService as a required parameter when it can be nil in some wirings (e.g., credits disabled)
Constructing BillingFeatureUnitCost{} struct literal instead of using FromBillingFeatureManualUnitCost/FromBillingFeatureLLMUnitCost setters

decisions.rationale: UnitCost is a discriminated union (manual/llm) requiring switch-based dispatch that Goverter cannot generate cleanly; hand-written code with tests is more explicit and caught by convert_test.go.

decisions.decision: LLM pricing resolved at read time, not stored
decisions.rationale: Pricing data changes independently of feature configuration; resolving at read time from llmcost.Service ensures freshness without coupling the feature write path to pricing.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new CRUD operation (e.g. ArchiveFeature) following the per-operation file pattern

api/v3/handlers/llmcost.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for LLM cost price catalog: listing system and manual prices, getting a price by ID, and managing per-namespace manual overrides (create, list, delete). Bridges llmcost.Service to HTTP using the standard httptransport pattern.

patterns.name: Decimal-as-string for pricing fields
patterns.description: All per-token pricing amounts are transmitted as api.Numeric strings and parsed/serialized via alpacadecimal. Use decimalFromString (wraps alpacadecimal.NewFromString + models.NewGenericValidationError) for all inbound conversions.
patterns.example: inputPerToken, err := decimalFromString(p.InputPerToken)

patterns.name: Provider display name mapping
patterns.description: providerDisplayNames map in convert.go provides canonical display names for known provider IDs. Unknown providers are title-cased by splitting on hyphen/underscore. Never hardcode provider display names in handler files.
patterns.example: Provider: api.LLMCostProvider{Id: providerID, Name: formatProviderName(providerID)}

patterns.name: Source mapping: internal vs API enum
patterns.description: Internal PriceSourceManual maps to api.LLMCostPriceSourceManual; everything else maps to api.LLMCostPriceSourceSystem. One-directional mapping (reads only expose system/manual).
patterns.example: source := api.LLMCostPriceSourceSystem; if p.Source == llmcost.PriceSourceManual { source = api.LLMCostPriceSourceManual }

patterns.name: Sort field allowlist validation
patterns.description: Validate sort fields against an explicit allowlist (validPriceSortField) before passing to the service. Return apierrors.NewBadRequestError with a descriptive Reason listing all valid fields.
patterns.example: if !validPriceSortField(sort.Field) { return req, apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, err, apierrors.InvalidParameters{{Field: "sort", Reason: "unsupported sort field, must be one of: id, provider.id, model.id, effective_from, effective_to"}}) }

patterns.name: Nil pointer optional pricing fields
patterns.description: Optional pricing fields (CacheReadPerToken, CacheWritePerToken, ReasoningPerToken) are *alpacadecimal.Decimal in domain and *string in API; use lo.ToPtr(d.String()) for non-nil values.
patterns.example: if p.CacheReadPerToken != nil { out.CacheReadPerToken = lo.ToPtr(p.CacheReadPerToken.String()) }

patterns.name: No sort support for list_overrides
patterns.description: Overrides do not support sort — do not add sort without first updating the service layer. list_prices.go supports sort; list_overrides.go does not — they are deliberately asymmetric.
patterns.example: // list_overrides.go has no sort params block — intentionally omitted

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (5 methods) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, llmcost.Service, options.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only one domain service (llmcost.Service) is injected; unlike features handler, no secondary services needed.

key_file_guides.role: All domain<->API conversions including domainPriceToAPI, apiPricingToDomain, apiCreateOverrideToDomain, provider display name formatting, and validPriceSortField.
key_file_guides.watch_for: decimalFromString wraps validation error; callers must propagate the error, not ignore it. Adding a new provider to providerDisplayNames requires updating the static map.

key_file_guides.role: Handles pagination, sort (with field allowlist), and filters (provider, model_id, model_name, currency, source) for the global price catalog.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Source filter uses filters.FromAPIFilterString, same as other string fields — not an enum filter. Sort field must pass validPriceSortField check.

key_file_guides.role: Handles pagination and filters (provider, model_id, model_name, currency) for per-namespace overrides. No sort support unlike list_prices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Overrides do not support sort — do not add sort without updating the service layer.

anti_patterns: Hardcoding provider display names in handler response logic instead of using formatProviderName from convert.go
Accepting decimal strings without using decimalFromString — bypasses validation error wrapping with models.NewGenericValidationError
Exposing internal PriceSource values directly to API instead of mapping through the source enum (system/manual)
Adding sort to list_overrides without updating the service layer to support it

decisions.decision: Provider display names in a static map with title-case fallback
decisions.rationale: API consumers need human-readable provider names; a central map avoids scatter while the fallback handles new/unknown providers without code changes.

decisions.decision: list_prices supports sort; list_overrides does not
decisions.rationale: Overrides are per-namespace and typically small; adding sort complexity would require service-layer changes not yet needed.

code_examples.scenario: Converting a domain Price to API response with optional pricing fields

api/v3/handlers/taxcodes.purpose: Full CRUD v3 HTTP handler package for billing tax codes (list, get, create, update/upsert, delete) plus organization-default tax code management. Uses Goverter-generated conversions (convert.gen.go) driven by annotated convert.go; includes custom AppType mapping (external_invoicing <-> custom_invoicing) for backwards compatibility.

patterns.name: Goverter variables pattern for conversions
patterns.description: convert.go declares package-level var functions annotated with goverter directives. convert.gen.go is generated and must never be hand-edited. The //go:generate directive at the top of convert.go regenerates it via 'make generate'.
patterns.example:
// goverter:variables
var FromAPICreateTaxCodeRequest func(namespace string, createTaxCodeRequest api.CreateTaxCodeRequest) (taxcode.CreateTaxCodeInput, error)

patterns.name: Backwards-compatible AppType mapping
patterns.description: FromAPIBillingAppType maps API value 'external_invoicing' to domain AppTypeCustomInvoicing. ToAPIBillingAppType maps domain AppTypeCustomInvoicing back to 'external_invoicing'. This pair must stay in sync.
patterns.example: func FromAPIBillingAppType(source api.BillingAppType) app.AppType { if source == "external_invoicing" { return app.AppTypeCustomInvoicing } ... }

patterns.name: Nil-to-empty-slice in ToAPIBillingTaxCodeAppMappings
patterns.description: Return []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping{} (not nil) when source is nil to prevent JSON null in AppMappings field.
patterns.example: func ToAPIBillingTaxCodeAppMappings(source taxcode.TaxCodeAppMappings) []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping { if source == nil { return []api.BillingTaxCodeAppMapping{} } ... }

patterns.name: Goverter context for namespace injection
patterns.description: Use goverter:context to pass namespace/namespacedID into generated converters. Implement NamespaceFromContext and ResolveNamespacedIDFromContext as context extractor functions referenced by goverter:map directives.

patterns.name: Upsert semantics for update endpoint
patterns.description: update.go uses UpsertTaxCodeRequest (not UpdateTaxCodeRequest) as the API body type — the endpoint is an upsert, not a partial update. Operation name is 'upsert-tax-code' not 'update-tax-code'.
patterns.example: httptransport.WithOperationName("upsert-tax-code")

key_file_guides.role: Source-of-truth for Goverter conversion declarations. Contains //go:generate directive, var declarations with goverter annotations, and hand-written helper functions (AppType mapping, nil-safe app mappings, reference ID extraction).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing annotations here requires running 'make generate' to regenerate convert.gen.go. Do NOT edit convert.gen.go directly.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Goverter from convert.go. Has '// Code generated by github.com/jmattheis/goverter, DO NOT EDIT.' header.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If this file is out of sync with convert.go annotations, builds produce stale/incorrect conversions. Regenerate with 'make generate'.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (7 methods including organization default tax code ops) and handler struct with resolveNamespace and taxcode.Service only.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No secondary service needed; taxcode.Service handles all CRUD including organization defaults.

key_file_guides.role: Uses UpsertTaxCodeRequest as the API body type — the endpoint is an upsert. Passes models.NamespacedID to FromAPIUpsertTaxCodeRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Operation name must match TypeSpec: 'upsert-tax-code'. Using api.UpdateTaxCodeRequest instead of api.UpsertTaxCodeRequest will break JSON deserialization.

anti_patterns: Editing convert.gen.go directly — regenerate via 'make generate' after modifying goverter annotations in convert.go
Forgetting the AppType backwards-compat mapping — 'external_invoicing' must round-trip correctly through both FromAPIBillingAppType and ToAPIBillingAppType
Returning nil for AppMappings instead of empty slice — causes JSON null which breaks API contract for clients expecting an array
Adding a new goverter:extend function in convert.go without also registering it in the global goverter:extend block at the top

decisions.decision: Goverter for tax code conversions instead of hand-written
decisions.rationale: Tax code conversion is straightforward field mapping without discriminated unions; Goverter reduces boilerplate while the context mechanism cleanly injects namespace.
The domain type was renamed from external_invoicing to custom_invoicing; the API preserves the old name for backwards compatibility with existing clients.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new Goverter conversion for a new request type following existing pattern
